query: design a plugin registry so new storage backends register themselves and the core never imports them
adding a backend means editing a giant switch in three files — make it so a new one plugs in without touching the core
rationale: open/closed via registry, not a central switch

query: should plug-ins be selected at build time, at startup from config, or at runtime per request?
rationale: binding time spectrum picks the mechanism

query: I want a fail-closed capability check so a plugin that lacks a guarantee is rejected without me editing the core
is passing my plugin registry into the services to look things up a bad idea?
rationale: service-locator anti-pattern; inject at the root instead

query: isolate my domain from the database behind a repository interface so I can swap it
grade how tightly these two modules are coupled and how to weaken the worst seam
which axis owns this concern and where should its directory go in the tree?
